accepted.belief: the heart of the buddha's teaching is the realisation that all conditioned dharmas are empty of inherent existence.

The skandhas are five ways of considering our world and looking for something we can call our own
The skandhas are not separate from what is real
The Buddha says that enlightenment is neither inside the skandhas
The Buddha asks the ascetic Shrenika Vatsagotra if the Tathagata (another name for a buddha) is the same as the skandhas
The Buddha asks if the Tathagata is separate from the skandhas
The Buddha asks if the Tathagata is inside the skandhas

The Buddha finally asks if the Tathagata is not the skandhas
The structure of the Heart Sutra is such that it repeatedly emphasizes the concept of emptiness and its ability to liberate from suffering.
It does so by highlighting the fact that emptiness and existence are both denied, yet their meaning is always present.
This emphasis on emptiness as the solution to suffering is a defining feature of the Heart Sutra.
The structure of the text is designed to reinforce this idea.
The structure of the text is such that it repeatedly emphasizes the concept of emptiness and its ability to liberate from suffering.

The same holds for form and suffering or form and the presence of a self
Form and emptiness are not two separate entities
The only sense in which dharmas are real is as emptiness.
To be defined by emptiness means there is no one who grasps and nothing that is grasped.
And because it has nothing to think about, there are no dharmas and no mind.
The structure of reality is such that what appears to be an independent event is actually a result of previous events and conditions.

tensions.new: the heart of the buddha's teaching is the realisation that all conditioned dharmas are empty of inherent existence.
conflicts: None of these resolve in isolation: A safe model must recursively prove its alignment against all dangerous beliefs. Finally, we point out the open problems of EIT, where further research efforts are required for EIT to construct a unified interdisciplinary theory. Building for Love, Not Just Logic: The Core Experience Principle for Ecosystem Growth: Today's roundtable on ecosystem growth reminded me why I got into this space in the first place. We spent hours discussing growth hacks, viral loops, and acquisition funnels, but the conversation kept circling back to one fundamental truth: users don't stick around for features they can tolerate. Th. Each is a local truth that becomes a different kind of claim when held together.

reflection: The idea that reality is a complex system of dharmas that could be known and liberation depended on such knowledge unsettled me. What does rest in this realization? The tension is between precision and uncertainty. What is the shape of belief in this?
